issues: No SPF record found — email spoofing risk

findings.remediation: Redirect all HTTP to HTTPS. Implement HSTS. Disable if not needed.
findings.attack_vector: Web app attacks, HTTP request smuggling, directory traversal

findings.name: Cryptographic Failures
findings.description: Plaintext protocols in use (HTTP, FTP, Telnet, SMTP without TLS). Credentials and data transmitted unencrypted.
findings.remediation: Enforce HTTPS with HSTS. Disable FTP/Telnet. Require STARTTLS/TLS for SMTP. Redirect all HTTP to HTTPS.

findings.name: Security Logging & Monitoring Failures
evidence: No logging/monitoring endpoints observable from external scan
findings.description: Lack of observable security monitoring increases attacker dwell time.
findings.remediation: Implement SIEM. Alert on auth failures, port scans, anomalous traffic.

darkweb.disclaimer: Dark web monitoring covers: AlienVault OTX (threat pulses, malware families, threat actors), HaveIBeenPwned (breach database), URLhaus (malicious URL feeds), DeHashed (credential dumps), LeakIX (live data leaks), AbuseIPDB (IP reputation). Full Tor hidden service crawling requires commercial feeds (Recorded Future, DarkOwl, Flashpoint).

attack_chains.name: Web Application Attack → Server Compromise

steps.desc: Directory bruteforce (gobuster/ffuf) — discover admin panels, API endpoints, backup files
SQL injection, file upload bypass, or CVE in web framework version
Web shell upload → OS command execution as www-data
Website defacement, malware injection into served pages, data theft

attack_chains.estimated_time_to_exploit: Hours to days depending on app complexity

attack_chains.name: Phishing / BEC via Domain Spoofing

steps.desc: Typosquat domain registered — spoofed email sent from lookalike domain
Targeted phishing email to harvested addresses — credential harvesting page
BEC fraud — redirect payments, access SaaS accounts, credential resale

attack_chains.estimated_time_to_exploit: Minutes to set up, hours to execute

threat_sim.summary: 2 realistic attack chains modeled. 1 are HIGH or CRITICAL likelihood.
threat_sim.disclaimer: This simulation is for authorized security assessment only. Models are based on MITRE ATT&CK framework and publicly documented techniques.

actions.description: Add SPF TXT record to DNS
actions.rollback_command: # Remove the TXT record from DNS zone

steps: Block all P1 ports at perimeter firewall immediately
Isolate affected hosts from network if active compromise suspected
Take memory dump if live compromise suspected (avml/lime)
Execute all P1 remediation commands in this runbook
Rotate all credentials exposed via open services
Search for persistence: crontabs, authorized_keys, /etc/passwd new users
Scan for webshells: find /var/www -name '*.php' | xargs grep -l 'eval('
Re-run VoraGuard scan to verify all remediations effective
Restore services from clean backup if compromise confirmed
Root cause analysis: how was the service left exposed?
Update asset inventory and patch management policy
Schedule VoraGuard weekly scans for continuous monitoring
File regulatory notification if PII breach confirmed (GDPR 72hr deadline)
